title: Avoid calling hashCode() and toString() on array instances
description: Calling hashCode() and toString() directly on array instances should be avoided because the default implementations provided by the Object class do not provide meaningful results for arrays. hashCode() returns the array's "identity hash code", and toString() returns nearly the same value. Neither method's output reflects the array's contents.
recommendation: Avoid calling hashCode() and toString() on array instances.
impact: The default implementations provided by the Object class do not provide meaningful results for arrays, which can lead to incorrect assumptions about the array's contents.

checks.message: Avoid calling hashCode() and toString() on array instances.
